summary.intent: Build and fix a browser viewing feature in the moltis project (Rex AI assistant), enabling users to see, create, and interact with browser sessions directly in the UI settings panel.
summary.outcome: Fully functional browser session UI with: null-stripping fix for LLM tool calls, shared BrowserManager between tool and service, New Session button with loading states, URL autocomplete with Google suggestions, working screencast via WebSocket subscription fix, mouse/keyboard/scroll interaction relay, and screenshot-on-select for instant session previews.

repo: BrowserTool and RealBrowserService previously had separate BrowserManager instances — sessions created by the AI tool were invisible to the UI API service
The v4 WebSocket protocol requires clients to explicitly subscribe to event types; browser.screencast.frame was missing from websocket.js subscription list causing all frames to be silently dropped
serde's #[serde(default)] handles missing fields but NOT explicit null values — LLMs (MiniMax) send null for every optional parameter, causing u64 deserialization failures
The screencast relay task exited when tx.send() found 0 receivers on first frame; CDP sends frames almost instantly before subscribers attach
RealBrowserService::request() defaults sandbox to false (unwrap_or(false)) while BrowserTool injects sandbox from the session router — UI-created sessions ran on host instead of sandbox
Screencast validate_url() only allowed http/https schemes, rejecting about:blank and causing silent failures for new sessions
The broadcast.rs filter at line 100-102 silently drops events for clients not subscribed to that event type

code.finding: Added obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null()) null-stripping before deserialization to handle LLM sending explicit nulls
Changed relay exit-on-no-receivers to drop-and-continue so CDP frames aren't lost before subscribers attach
Subscription filter silently drops events for unsubscribed clients — all new event types must be added to websocket.js subscription list
browser.screencast.frame was missing from the subscribeEvents list, causing all screencast frames to be dropped at broadcast layer
Added default_sandbox flag to RealBrowserService to inject sandbox:true into requests when sandbox mode is enabled
Added parameterized integration tests for screencast chain across host/sandbox modes; sandbox tests auto-skip when runtime unavailable

workflow: Diagnostic logging at each relay stage (CDP → ScreencastRegistry → broadcast → WS client) was essential for pinpointing the exact break point
Writing an integration test that passed (host mode frames DO arrive) proved the break was downstream in the gateway layer, not CDP
YubiKey signing required interactive tap for git push — push commands need to be retried after tap
biome for JS linting and cargo fmt/clippy for Rust should be run before each commit
Tailwind CSS rarely needs rebuilding when using existing CSS variables and utility classes

friction: Multiple push failures requiring YubiKey tap — no way to pre-authorize or batch sign

Silent failures throughout: validate_url rejecting about:blank with success:false (200 OK), broadcast dropping unsubscribed events, screencast relay exiting silently — none surfaced errors to the user
Race condition between CDP sending first frame and subscriber attaching required multiple debug iterations to identify
The broken screencast required 5+ debug rounds across different layers (null deserialization → relay exit → navigate URL validation → WS subscription → broadcast filter) before working end-to-end
pre-existing clippy errors in moltis-voice and CUDA crates caused noise during linting of unrelated crates
open_items: Per-agent browser profiles — currently a single global profile dir; no isolation between agent sessions
Google suggestion autocomplete uses suggestqueries.google.com which may have CORS limitations in sandbox
Paused session indicator and re-activation flow (started but not fully polished per last transcript message)
Fetching/loading state during screenshot REST call (last message in transcript — started but not committed)
Playwright e2e tests were written but not validated against a running server; screencast frame delivery test may be flaky
The sandbox mode default in RealBrowserService reads config.tools.exec.sandbox.mode as a string comparison — fragile if SandboxMode enum changes
No rate limiting or debouncing on mouse/scroll events relayed to CDP — could cause performance issues with high-frequency scroll
